metadata.seed: I was already having a really bad day, from a run in I had with a very rude lady. Then I received a text from someone who I thought was my friend. She felt like I haven' t been there for her because I didn't pick up her call. I honestly didn't see her call and definitely would've got back to her, if I saw her call. She said mean and nasty things about me, which I don' t care about. But she did the wrong mistake of bringing my child into the equation, when he had nothing to do with it. I wanted to really beat the crap out of her because not only did she do that, she blocked my number and ran. Like I was beyond pissed.

patient.query: I was already having a really bad day, from a run in I had with a very rude lady. Then I received a text from someone who I thought was my friend. She felt like I haven' t been there for her because I didn't pick up her call. I honestly didn't see her call and definitely would've got back to her, if I saw her call. She said mean and nasty things about me, which I don' t care about. But she did the wrong mistake of bringing my child into the equation, when he had nothing to do with it. I wanted to really beat the crap out of her because not only did she do that, she blocked my number and ran. Like I was beyond pissed.

triggers: run in with a rude lady
text from someone I thought was my friend
mean and nasty things said about me
my child being brought into the equation
automatic_thoughts: I didn't see her call
I would have got back to her if I saw her call
she did the wrong mistake
I wanted to beat the crap out of her

neutral: Worried about being a bad mother (finding)

I can understand why you might feel angry when someone brings your child into an argument that doesn't involve them. Let's take some time to explore these emotions and thoughts together, so we can better understand what's been happening for you.

Like, here I was, just trying to figure things out with my friends, and then suddenly, it's all mixed up with something that shouldn't even involve my child. It just made me feel... I don't know, like my boundaries were crossed or something.

patient.query: Yeah, it was pretty overwhelming. I felt really exposed and vulnerable. It’s just like… I thought we were on the same page, and then bam, something completely out of the blue. It made me question everything—like, do they really care about my feelings? Am I just supposed to be okay with whatever they throw my way?

patient.query: Yeah, I suppose we could... I mean, growing up, I often felt like my thoughts didn’t really matter, like I had to just go along with what everyone else wanted. It’s like an echo, you know? So when it comes to my friends and expressing myself now, I get this nagging voice in my head saying it’s better to just stay quiet. But then it feels really isolating.

automatic_thoughts: my thoughts don’t matter
I have to go along with what everyone else wants
it’s better to just stay quiet

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

Sometimes, I think it’d be easier if I just kept quiet. But then I end up feeling so alone in all of it, like I’m just pretending everything's fine when it’s not.

patient.query: Thanks, I appreciate that. It really does feel tough, like I’m stuck between wanting to open up and just shutting down to avoid the risk. The fear makes it hard to even know where to start when I think about what to say to them. Sometimes, I feel so frustrated because I want connection but I feel like I’m building a wall at the same time.

I might get hurt
I can't express myself
I'm building a wall

patient.query: Yeah, I think so. I mean, I want to be able to tell them how I really feel without feeling like I’m just going to crumble or that it’ll blow up in my face. A good conversation would be... I guess open and honest, where they actually listen and understand me. But then I immediately think, “What if they don’t respond well?” It’s like I have this little voice that always jumps in to remind me how things have gone wrong before.

I’ll crumble
